nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required. PACE Student by Permission and Override

nodes.title: Junior Honors College Seminar
nodes.description: Appropriate for interdepartmental and interdisciplinary topics in Agriculture and Life Sciences. Permission of Dean's Office.
nodes.other_requirements: Permission of Dean's Office. Instructor permission required

nodes.title: 1st year Honors Colloquium
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Communication Methods
nodes.description: Introduction to informational and persuasive public speaking. Developing individual and group oral communication skills through giving and critically analyzing presentations.

nodes.title: Foundation:Information Tech
nodes.description: Foundational course to acclimate College of Agriculture & Life Science First-Year students to college life and develop information technology skills through use of computer hardware and software and internet applications.
nodes.other_requirements: CALS First-Year, sophomore and PACE students only; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Int:Self Design Major Proposal
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; PACE Student by Permission and Override Students who successfully complete and present their proposals for the CALS Self Design Major program can register for this 1-credit experience. Students work with the CALS Self Design Major Program Director and their Academic Advisor to prepare a complete written proposal and deliver a presentation to the Faculty Review Committee.

nodes.title: Computer Applications
nodes.description: Use of computer operating systems programming languages, electronic communications, word processing, spreadsheet modeling and graphics, and internet software related to the agricultural and life sciences.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistant Development
nodes.description: TAs develop skills in areas of leadership, group dynamics, interpersonal effectiveness, and assertiveness as group facilitators in Beginnings course. Prerequisite: Sophomore standing only; Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Sophomore standing only; Instructor permission. Instructor permission required PACE Student by Permission and Override

nodes.title: Foundations:Communication Meth
nodes.description: Foundational course to acclimate College of Agriculture & Life Science First-Year students to college life and develop individual and group public speaking skills through giving and critically analyzing presentations.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistant-Info Tech
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory-level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; PACE Student by Permission and Override
